reasoning: Even though the EPA's Office of Atmospheric Protection is slated for elimination and the Energy Star program is being de-prioritized according to May 2025 news, the timeline until July 1, 2025 is extremely short (less than two months). Furthermore, the Energy Star program is actually established by statute (Energy Policy Act of 2005, codified at 42 U.S.C. § 6294a), meaning the administration cannot completely abolish the program without congressional action. It is highly likely the domain will either be transferred to the DOE, stripped down to a minimal statutory version, or redirected to an archive—all of which would result in the domain technically responding to pings/HTTP requests and thus being marked as 'UP' by 'Is It Down Right Now?'. The short timeframe and bureaucratic inertia strongly push toward the site still being reachable on July 1.
